channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: British Army conducts secret Nato exercise in London Underground highlighting drone shortfall
item.description: Deep within the disused terminus of Charing Cross station, a secret Nato command bunker has hosted a British-led military exercise simulating a Russian attack on Estonia in 2030. Known as Arrcade Stri...

item.title: New Balance’s Grey Days Campaign Captures Johannesburg Winter Style
item.description: As Johannesburg settles into winter, New Balance’s Grey Days campaign has struck a chord with the city’s seasonal fashion mood. Winter in Jozi encourages thoughtful layering, stylish comfort, and a fo...

item.title: Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie’s 2006 Motorcycle Visit to Ho Chi Minh City
item.description: In November 2006, Hollywood stars Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie visited Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, in a surprising and low-key manner that contrasted sharply with typical celebrity trips. Flying in fro...

item.title: Guidelines and Risks for Traveling with Medical Marijuana by Air
item.description: Traveling with medical marijuana in the U.S. has become somewhat more legally recognized, but complications remain. The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) recently updated its website to ref...

item.title: American Skateboarder Jason Vanporppal Nears Completion of Africa-Wide Skateboarding Journey for Charity
item.description: American skateboarder Jason Vanporppal is close to completing a remarkable journey from Kampala, Uganda to Cape Town, South Africa, covering over 6,400 kilometres across seven African countries. Begin...

item.title: Chris Obure’s Company Sues for KSh 7.6 Billion after Alleged Forceful Eviction from Lenana Road Office
item.description: A Nairobi-based aviation and real estate firm owned by city tycoon Chris Obure has filed a case at the High Court seeking KSh 7.6 billion in compensation, claiming it was unlawfully evicted from prime...

item.title: Major Lotto Jackpots Remain Unclaimed on May 22, 2026
item.description: On Friday, May 22, 2026, no bettors won the top prizes in the major lottery draws organized by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The Ultra Lotto 6/58 jackpot of P75,000,000.00 went unc...

item.title: PSG’s Hakimi Seeks Court Dismissal in Pending Rape Case
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi, 27, wey dey face trial for alleged rape, don ask court make dem dismiss the case. The matter dey before Versailles appeals court, west of Paris. The case na...

item.title: FG reports 2.1 million pregnant women benefiting from health sector reforms
item.description: The federal government of Nigeria don report say over 2.1 million pregnant women dey access antenatal care under the new health sector reforms wey dem dey implement through the Nigeria Health Sector R...

item.title: Jamie Bell Discusses His Role in Richard Gadd’s Intense Drama Half Man
item.description: Jamie Bell speaks candidly about his starring role in Richard Gadd’s six-part BBC series Half Man, a drama exploring toxic masculinity and complex male relationships. Bell portrays Niall, a bookish, c...

item.title: London man sentenced to five years for antisemitic threats and offences
item.description: A 36-year-old man, Tavius Jean-Charles, has been sentenced to five years in prison in London for a series of antisemitic hate crimes. Between October 2025 and March 2026, Jean-Charles targeted six ind...

item.title: Guide to Unlocking the Free Ultron Cybernetic Drip Skin in Marvel Rivals’ Savage Adventure Event
item.description: Marvel Rivals has launched a new event called Savage Adventure as part of its Season 8 content update. The event features a free Ultron Cybernetic Drip skin, which players can unlock by reaching Tier ...

item.title: Pentagon publishes second set of declassified UFO reports and witness accounts
item.description: The Pentagon has issued a second batch of declassified UFO files, weeks after the first release, which have attracted widespread public attention. The documents are available on the Pentagon’s officia...

item.title: Alberta Premier Smith proposes adding a separation question to fall referendum
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has announced plans to respond to the province's upcoming referendum by including a question on whether Alberta should pursue a separation referendum. Smith emphasized t...

item.title: How South Africa’s Property Market is Evolving in 2026
item.description: South Africa’s property sector is undergoing significant transformation driven by economic shifts, technological advances and changing lifestyle demands. Self-storage, once a basic service, is now eme...

item.title: Dutch Warship Visits Manila Amid Chinese Navy Helicopter Approach
item.description: A Dutch naval ship, His Netherlands Majesty’s Ship de Ruyter, was approached by a Chinese navy helicopter while en route to Manila. Commander Rodger De Wit of the Dutch ship described the encounter as...

item.title: Iconic 1987 Portraits of Dolly Parton by Photographer Mario Casilli
item.description: In 1987, legendary photographer Mario Casilli captured a striking series of portraits of Dolly Parton, which became iconic representations of the singer’s 1980s public persona. These images were prima...

item.title: Pakistan and Turkiye Strengthen Defence and Aerospace Collaboration
item.description: The Pakistan Air Force (PAF) and the Turkiye Air Force have reinforced their strategic partnership in defence, aerospace innovation, and emerging technologies, the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISP...

item.title: CIDG assesses Senate security gunfire response as excessive
item.description: The Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) has described the recent gunfire incident involving Senate security personnel as 'overkill.' While the circumstances surrounding the event remain ...

item.title: Bato dela Rosa Says He Only Hitched a Ride with Padilla; ICC Denies Duterte Delay Attempt
item.description: On May 22, 2026, key developments in Philippine politics and global affairs were reported. Senator Robinhood Padilla clarified that Senator Bato dela Rosa simply asked for a ride when leaving the Sena...
